info.title: The Drive AI API
info.description:
## The Drive AI API

One API for screenshots, markdown extraction, structured data extraction,
and more. 107+ file formats supported.

## Endpoints

| Endpoint | Credits | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `GET /{url}` | 1 | Screenshot any URL |
| `GET /md/{url}` | 1 | Convert any URL to markdown |
| `POST /api/v1/thumbnails/generate` | 1 | Generate thumbnail from file or URL |
| `POST /api/v1/markdown/convert` | 1 | Convert file/URL to markdown |
| `POST /api/v1/extract` | 1/page or 5/site | Extract structured data |
| `POST /api/v1/analyze` | 2/page or 10/site | Analyze — compute, reason, derive |

## Authentication

All protected endpoints require an `X-API-Key` header:
```bash
curl https://dev.thedrive.ai/stripe.com \
  -H "X-API-Key: tda_live_your_key_here"
```

Get your API key at https://dev.thedrive.ai/login

## Pricing

- **Free**: 100 credits/month, no card required
- **Pro**: $0.01/credit, pay-as-you-go
- **Enterprise**: Volume pricing, contact us

post.description:
Generate a thumbnail from a file upload or URL.

**File Upload**: Use multipart/form-data with a `file` field.

**URL Fetch**: Use JSON body with `url` field. Optionally include `url_headers` for authenticated URLs.

**Options**:
- `width`, `height`: Max dimensions (default 600x400)
- `quality`: JPEG quality 1-100 (default 95)
- `sync`: Wait for result (true) or return job ID (false)
- `webhook_url`: URL to POST result when complete (async only)
- `response_type`: "url" returns storage URL, "base64" returns encoded image
Generate a thumbnail from a URL using JSON request body.

get.description: Get the status of an async thumbnail generation job.

delete.description: Delete a job and its associated thumbnail.

schema.title: Response Delete Job Api V1 Thumbnails Jobs  Job Id  Delete

get.description: Get the raw thumbnail image bytes.
Get a list of all supported file types grouped by handler.

Generate and return a thumbnail image directly from a URL. **Usage**: Perfect for embedding in `` tags or direct browser access. ```html ``` **Parameters**: - `url`: The URL of the file to generate a thumbnail from (required) - `width`, `height`: Max dimensions (default 600x400) - `quality`: JPEG quality 1-100 (default 95) **Returns**: Raw JPEG image bytes with `Content-Type: image/jpeg`

post.description:
Convert a file or URL to markdown.

**File Upload**: Use multipart/form-data with a `file` field.

**URL Fetch**: Provide a `url` field. For websites (HTML), uses a headless browser
to render the page before converting — handles JS-heavy sites.

**Supported formats**: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, CSV, HTML, images, audio,
code files, and any URL via headless browser rendering.

get.description: List all file types supported for markdown conversion.

post.description:
Analyze and cross-reference multiple documents together. Unlike `/analyze` (single
document) or `/analyze/batch` (many documents independently), this endpoint lets
the agent reason **across** all documents simultaneously.

**Use cases**:
- Validate an invoice against a contract or purchase order
- Check that a report's numbers match a source spreadsheet
- Compare terms across multiple agreements
- Reconcile data from different sources

Upload 2-5 files and/or provide URLs. Each document gets a label (auto-generated
from filename, or you can provide custom labels). The agent can search, read, and
compare data across all documents.

Provide a typed schema — each field has a type and description.

**Example**:
```json
{
  "rates_match": {"type": "boolean", "description": "Do the hourly rates on the invoice match the contract?"},
  "total_valid": {"type": "boolean", "description": "Does the invoice total equal the sum of line items?"},
  "correct_vendor": {"type": "boolean", "description": "Is the vendor name on the invoice the same as the contracting party?"}
}
```

**Pricing**: 5 credits/document + 3 credits/page (minimum 10).

Analyze a document by asking questions. Unlike `/extract`, this endpoint
can **compute, reason, and derive** values that aren't explicitly written
in the document.

Upload a file (or provide a URL) along with a typed schema. Each field
must have a `type` and `description`.

**Example schema**:
```json
{
  "total_cost": {"type": "number", "description": "Sum all line item amounts"},
  "is_auto_renew": {"type": "boolean", "description": "Does this contract auto-renew?"},
  "risk_level": {"type": "string", "enum": ["low", "medium", "high"], "description": "Legal risk level"}
}
```

**Supported types**: `string`, `number`, `integer`, `boolean`, `array`, `object`

Every answer includes:
- The computed answer (type-enforced)
- Reasoning explaining how it was derived
- Source text snippets from the document
- Confidence level

**Pricing**: 2 credits/page (min 5) for documents, 10 credits for websites.

Submit multiple files or URLs for extraction with a single schema.
Each item is processed concurrently. Returns a batch_id to poll.

**Poll**: `GET /api/v1/jobs/batch/{batch_id}`

**Limits**: Maximum 20 items per batch.

Submit multiple files or URLs for analysis with a single schema.
Each item is processed concurrently. Returns a batch_id to poll.

**Poll**: `GET /api/v1/jobs/batch/{batch_id}`

**Limits**: Maximum 10 items per batch.

get.description: Poll the status of an async extract or analyze task. Returns the result when complete.
Poll the status of a batch operation. Returns all results when complete.

Check the delivery status of webhooks for a task. Returns a list of
delivery attempts with status, response code, and timestamps.

Useful for debugging "I didn't receive my webhook" scenarios.
Check the health status of the service.

schema.title: Response Liveness Api V1 Health Live Get
Response Readiness Api V1 Health Ready Get

get.description:
Convert any URL to markdown by prepending `/md/`.

**Usage Examples:**
```
# Website
dev.thedrive.ai/md/example.com

# PDF document
dev.thedrive.ai/md/arxiv.org/pdf/1706.03762

# Any file URL
dev.thedrive.ai/md/example.com/report.docx
```

Returns plain text markdown. Websites are rendered with a headless browser
so JS-heavy pages work too.

Generate a thumbnail by simply appending a URL to the service path. **Usage Examples:** ``` # Without scheme (https:// is assumed) dev.thedrive.ai/example.com/document.pdf # With full URL dev.thedrive.ai/https://example.com/image.png # With options dev.thedrive.ai/example.com/doc.pdf?w=300&h=200&q=80 # URLs with query strings (e.g., Azure SAS tokens) dev.thedrive.ai/https://storage.blob.core.windows.net/file.pdf?sv=2024&sig=xxx ``` **In HTML:** ```html ``` **Query Parameters (ours):** - `w`: Width (default 600, max 2000) - `h`: Height (default 400, max 2000) - `q`: Quality (default 95, 1-100) - `nocache`: Bypass cache (default false) **Screenshot Options (websites only, ignored for documents):** - `fullpage`: Capture full scrollable page (default false) - `delay`: Extra wait in ms after page load, max 5000 (default 0) - `dark`: Emulate dark mode / prefers-color-scheme: dark (default false) - `device`: Viewport preset — `mobile` (390x844), `tablet` (820x1180), `desktop` (1440x900) - `viewport`: Raw viewport override, e.g. `375x812` (overrides device) - `selector`: CSS selector to capture a specific element (falls back to viewport if not found) - `wait`: CSS selector to wait for before capturing (max 5s timeout) **Note:** Query parameters from the target URL (like Azure SAS tokens) are preserved and passed through.

reasoning.rates_match: Invoice hourly rate ($150) differs from contract rate ($125)

label.title: Label
label.description: Document label used in the analysis

total_pages.title: Total Pages

DocumentInfo.title: DocumentInfo
DocumentInfo.description: Metadata about one document in a cross-document analysis.

success.title: Success
success.description: True if all required fields were found. False if any required field is missing (partial data still returned).

data.title: Data
data.description: Extracted structured data matching the provided schema. Types are guaranteed when using typed schema.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: Per-field calibrated confidence scores (0.0–1.0). Composite of LLM self-report, token logprobs, and OCR quality weighted by extraction method.

citations.title: Citations
citations.description: Per-field source text snippets from the document

model_used.title: Model Used
model_used.description: Model used for extraction (fast or accurate)

extraction_method.title: Extraction Method
extraction_method.description: How text was extracted: text, ocr, ocr+vision, vision, or dom+text

pages_processed.title: Pages Processed
pages_processed.description: Number of document pages actually read (0 for non-paginated files)

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total pages in the document (0 for non-paginated files)

schema_format.description: Schema format: always 'typed'. Each field has an explicit type for guaranteed type conformance.

missing_required.title: Missing Required
missing_required.description: List of required fields that could not be found (typed schemas only). Present only when success=false.

quality_signals.title: Quality Signals
quality_signals.description: Raw quality signals used for confidence calibration. 'ocr_quality': blended OCR quality (0.7*mean + 0.3*p10, 0–1, absent for non-OCR). 'doc_quality': geometric mean of LLM token probs (0–1). 'signal_disagreement': mean |llm_self_report - logprob| across fields. Values above 0.3 indicate the signals disagree — the model may be confidently wrong (high logprob, low self-report) or self-deprecating.

required.title: Required
required.description: Whether the field was marked required

FieldStatusValue.title: FieldStatusValue
FieldStatusValue.description: Whether a field was found during extraction.

ThumbnailRequest.title: ThumbnailRequest
ThumbnailRequest.description:
Request model for thumbnail generation.

Either `url` must be provided (for URL-based fetching) or a file must be
uploaded via multipart form data. When using the JSON body, only `url` is supported.

ThumbnailResponse.title: ThumbnailResponse
ThumbnailResponse.description: Response for a completed thumbnail generation.
